title: Hraunhafnartangi Lighthouse
summary: Hraunhafnartangi Lighthouse (Icelandic: Hraunhafnartangaviti [틛r퍈콉yn틟hapnar틟t퉗au콂ka틟v톩틣t톩], regionally also [-틟v톩틣t퉗톩]) is the northernmost lighthouse in Iceland.  It is located about 800 meters south of the Arctic Circle.
text:
Hraunhafnartangi Lighthouse (Icelandic: Hraunhafnartangaviti [틛r퍈콉yn틟hapnar틟t퉗au콂ka틟v톩틣t톩], regionally also [-틟v톩틣t퉗톩]) is the northernmost lighthouse in Iceland.  It is located about 800 meters south of the Arctic Circle.

Location and description
The lighthouse is on the northeast coast of Iceland, about 10 km northwest of the town of Raufarh칬fn, the northernmost town in Iceland.  The light tower is square, 19 metres tall and built of concrete.  The lantern house is painted red.

Light characteristic
The light characteristic is a long and a short flash every 30 seconds.  The flash is white or red depending on the direction from the lighthouse.
